twitter_thread: 25% of software budgets are now spent on AI, says IBM. Expect 30% of projects to be put on hold.
Average AI project cost: $1.2m. Median team size: 10 people. Salaries: $125k-$175k.
TensorFlow and PyTorch power 70% of AI projects, but customization costs add up fast.
Google Cloud AI Platform and AWS SageMaker can cut costs by up to 40%.
Hiring a full-time AI engineer costs $150k/yr. Freelancers: $75/hr.
Using open-source AI tools without customization can lead to 20% project failure rate.
How do you prioritize AI spending without starving other software projects? #aiinvesting #softwarebudgets
pack.linkedin: IBM's warning that AI is consuming 25% of software budgets has significant implications. With average AI project costs at $1.2m and team salaries ranging from $125k to $175k, it's clear that budgets are being stretched. Cloud-based AI services like Google Cloud AI Platform can reduce costs by up to 40%. What strategies are you using to balance AI investment with other software costs?
tiktok_hooks: Your AI project could be 20% doomed
Cutting AI costs by 40% is possible
pack.reddit_headline: Is the AI boom bankrupting the software industry?
